_note: ORIGINAL content. javascript.info and Eloquent JavaScript informed topic coverage only; no prose, examples, or exercises were copied.
title: Conditionals
summary: Select one path with ordered boolean tests.
refresher: An `if` branch runs when its condition is truthy. Add `else if` for another test and `else` for remaining cases. Put narrower overlapping rules first.

blocks.body: An `if` branch runs when its condition is truthy. Add `else if` for another test and `else` for remaining cases. Put narrower overlapping rules first. Select one path with ordered boolean tests.

A conditional chain is read from top to bottom. The first truthy condition wins, and the rest of that chain is skipped. Truthy does not mean equal to `true`; nonempty strings and most objects are truthy, while zero, an empty string, null, undefined, and NaN are falsy.

blocks.caption: The chain stops after the first true threshold.
Place specific rules before broad rules.

blocks.body: Trace a score of 95 through both versions. In the wrong order, 95 matches the broad passing rule and the chain stops. In the corrected order, it reaches the A rule first. Boundary tests such as 59, 60, 89, and 90 reveal ordering mistakes quickly.

blocks.title: Check the boundary
blocks.body: Writing `if (status = "ready")` assigns text instead of comparing it; use strict equality for a comparison.

blocks.code:
const n=0;
if(n) console.log("yes"); else console.log("no");
blocks.why: Zero is falsy, so execution moves to the else branch.
The chain stops at its first match, so a broad rule can hide a narrower grade range.
